B.title: EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
B.inline_verdict: Compelling mix-shift trade with genuine asymmetry; DC moat YELLOW, M&A ORANGE — ESA clears for starter position

blocks.text: ESA confirms the Triaging thesis with higher conviction. Hexatronic's DC segment is real, scaling, and profitable (Q4-25: +62% organic, 15.3% EBITA, asset-light). The hyperscaler AI buildout is a multi-year structural tailwind confirmed by Corning's $6bn Meta contract. HTRO's mid-market niche (Tier-2 cloud, regional DCs, enterprise) is less competed than Tier-1 hyperscaler direct supply. ESA-depth research confirms: (i) DC moat is narrow but defensible via local service execution + installed base stickiness — YELLOW, not ORANGE; (ii) M&A execution remains the binding constraint — Weterings write-off is a flag, but Fröberg's Ahlsell track record (20+ deals) is relevant. Overall IC strengthens from C+ to C+ (upper end). Starter position (1.0-1.5%) is justified at current entry (~SEK 28). Do NOT size up before Q1-26 DC print confirms.

ESA-depth analysis adds three layers beyond Triaging: (1) Sector primer confirms fibre optic industry is bifurcating between vertically integrated giants (Corning, Prysmian — preform + cable) and service-led integrators (HTRO niche). Preform manufacturers capture 70% of industry profit pool — HTRO cannot access this. But HTRO's 15%+ DC services margin is competitive with system integrator peers (~11.5% average). (2) Value chain analysis shows HTRO is deliberately moving downstream (design → install → day-two services) where switching costs are higher and competition from giants is less intense. The Communication Zone acquisition exemplifies this — local service capability in US Midwest for hyperscale clients. (3) Earnings history reveals a pattern: management under-promises after misses (H1-25) then over-delivers (H2-25). Q4-25 EBITA beat +10.8% is consistent with conservative guidance reset. This pattern is favourable for EPS revision momentum — expect continued positive surprises if DC sustains.

items.text: PRIMARY driver: DC segment scaling — fulcrum. Q4-25 organic growth +62% confirms momentum; 15.3% EBITA margin is 2x group average.

sub.text: DC + HE = ~40% of sales but ~60% of adj. EBITA by Q4-25. Mix shift is the numbers story.
Cloud/hyperscaler = 40% of DC sales; enterprise = 60%. Dual customer base reduces single-customer risk.
Management targets 3-6 DC acquisitions/year at 5-7x EBITDA, EV <EUR 50m. Pipeline appears real given US market fragmentation.

items.text: SECONDARY driver: BEAD programme materialisation — triggers legacy FTTH volume recovery in H2-26.

sub.text: $42.5bn US subsidy; 50 of 56 state proposals approved. 74% of early allocations still going to fibre despite technology-neutral rule change.
BEAD impact timing has been pushed right repeatedly. Management now guides H2-26; sell-side expects 2027 for meaningful revenue contribution.

items.text: TERTIARY driver: Harsh Environment defence/offshore tailwind — structural, secular, non-cyclical ballast.

sub.text: HE segment: +11% organic 2025, 11.2% EBITA margin rising toward 15% target. JOWO acquisition (EUR 11.8m, 4.7x EBITDA) adds European defence exposure.
NATO spending commitments provide 5-10 year visibility. Rochester Cable operational improvements are margin-accretive.

blocks.text: Key risk: the DC boom-bust parallel. Sell-side (Redeye) explicitly warns that hyperscaler capex could exhibit the same boom-bust as FTTH. The FTTH cycle saw Hexatronic's revenue surge 33% CAGR then collapse — if DC follows the same pattern, the 2028 targets are unachievable. Mitigant: DC business is services-led (design, installation, day-two) vs. FTTH which was product-distribution; services have higher switching costs and recurring revenue characteristics. This distinction needs pressure-testing at ESA.

blocks.text: _Technical picture is constructive but not confirmed. Stage 1→2 transition is the key technical catalyst. Monitor MAs for golden cross confirmation._
_DC segment is the thesis. Mix shift from FTTH (6.4% margin) to DC (15.3% margin) is the EPS engine. Change forces confirmed; moat question outstanding._
Change forces confirmed and large. DC organic +37% FY25, +62% Q4. Dual setup: Demand-Driven EPSU/EPT (primary) + Corporate Change (secondary). Conviction C+ upper.

EPS path plausibly present. Triple ratchet: volume (DC scaling +62% Q4 organic) + margin (mix shift from 5% FS to 15% DC) + multiple (re-rate from 12.5x to 18-20x). Modal case: FY28 EPS SEK 3.30-3.60 vs FY25 ~SEK 1.62. Conviction C+ upper.

items.text: **IC#1 Required Case OUTPUTS:** Triple ratchet path is plausible but back-loaded. DC organic growth trajectory (39% Q3, 62% Q4 2025) combined with HE +11% and FS cost programme creates credible

sub.text: path to 20%+ EPS CAGR 2025-28. Key constraint: M&A execution in DC must deliver without margin dilution.

items.text: **IC#1 RA1 — Sufficient longevity of case (baton)?** Yes, conditionally. DC megatrend (hyperscaler capex, AI infrastructure) has multi-year runway. Management 2028 targets imply 3Y+ execution horizon. Risk:

sub.text: Fibre Solutions drag could shorten the window if it absorbs management attention.

items.text: **IC#1 CQ1 — Three-year triple ratchet step-up:** Plausibly present. DC organic growth trajectory (39% Q3, 62% Q4 2025) combined with HE +11% and Fibre Solutions cost programme creates a credible

sub.text: path to 20%+ EPS CAGR over 2025-28.
VF modal case: FY28 EPS SEK 3.30-3.60 vs FY25 ~SEK 1.62. Triple ratchet requires sustained DC scaling + mix shift + cost savings.
Management 2028 targets: SEK 11bn revenue, 12% EBITA margin. SS consensus lower at SEK 9.3bn / 9.9% margin — gap is M&A-dependent.

items.text: **IC#1 RA2 — Sufficient growth rate?** Yes on modal case. SS consensus implies ~20-25% EPS CAGR 2025-28. VF case sits 12-15% above SS.

sub.text: Comfortably in GARP sweet spot if DC growth sustains above 30%.

items.text: **IC#1 CQ2 — 12-20% EPS growth pa:** Achievable on modal case. SS consensus implies ~20-25% EPS CAGR 2025-28. VF case sits 12-15% above SS. GARP sweet spot.

sub.text: Growth mix: ~60% organic DC/HE, ~25% M&A, ~15% cost savings. EPS acceleration is back-loaded (mix shift compounds).

items.text: **IC#1 RA3 — Sufficient improvement in financials?** Margin trajectory is the key question. EBITA margin fell from 15.1% (2023) to 9.4% (2025).

sub.text: Recovery path depends on mix shift toward DC (15.3% margin) and HE (11.4%) while FS stabilises at 5-6%. PIP savings (SEK 110m/yr) provide floor.

items.text: **IC#1 CQ3 — Margin/growth step-up:** Present but early. Gross margin compressed from 42.2% (2023) to 38.5% (2025). Recovery requires DC mix accretion. DC at 15.3% EBITA vs group 9.4% —

sub.text: every 5pp of mix shift toward DC adds ~50bp to group margin. PIP adds ~150bp (SEK 110m on SEK 7.5bn revenue).
Segment margins Q4 2025: DC 15.3%, HE 11.4%, FS 5.2%. Group target 12% by 2028 requires DC to reach ~35% of revenue (from 20%).
Margin step-up is credible but back-loaded. 2026 likely ~10-11% EBITA (PIP run-rate + DC growth). 2027-28 inflection toward 12%+ if M&A integrates well.

items.text: **IC#1 RA4 — Sufficient setup fit?** Good fit with Demand-Driven EPSU/EPT. DC hyperscaler buildout is a large, identifiable exogenous demand shift with multi-year visibility.

sub.text: Secondary Corporate Change setup (CEO + restructuring) adds conviction layer.

items.text: **IC#1 CQ4 — Fit with required setups:** Demand-Driven EPSU/EPT is the primary fit. DC hyperscaler buildout = large, identifiable exogenous demand shift. Secondary: Corporate Change (CEO + restructuring).

sub.text: Both setups are legible.
Primary setup: DC bandwidth demand +330% (2020-24). Hyperscaler capex acceleration (AI/ML). HTRO positioned as infrastructure enabler with 62% organic DC growth confirming demand pull.
Secondary setup: New CEO Fröberg (Mar 2025) + PIP + M&A strategy reset. Corporate change is real but execution risk remains (20+ acquisitions 2020-24 need integration).

items.text: **IC#1 RA5 — Goldilocks timeliness?** Mixed. SP has rallied +42% YTD from SEK 16.44 low to ~SEK 31.50. Not a trough entry.

sub.text: But DC growth re-acceleration (Q4 +62%) may signal the start of a multi-quarter upgrade cycle. Timeliness is acceptable if conviction is on 2-3Y horizon, not 6M trade.

items.text: **IC#1 CQ5 — Post CfC clearing / SP turn?** Partially. Major CfC was Fibre Solutions demand collapse (2024) + CEO exit. SP bottomed at SEK 16.44 (2024 low) and has

sub.text: recovered to ~SEK 31.50. CfC partially cleared: FS stabilising, new CEO installed. Residual CfC: FS margin floor uncertain, North America challenged.
52-week range: 16.44-34.94 SEK. Current price ~90% of 52-week high. Most of the CfC clearing is priced in at the SP level.
Not a classic post-CfC entry. More of a 'growth re-acceleration at reasonable price' setup. Acceptable if conviction is on 2-3Y EPS compounding, not CfC clearing bounce.

items.text: **IC#1 CQ6 — Less than 6M after turn?** No. SP turn was mid-2024 (~SEK 16-18 range). We are 12-18M post-turn. This is not an early-stage timeliness entry.

sub.text: Acceptable only if the next leg (DC growth confirmation + margin inflection) is a separate catalyst window.
SP turn began ~Jun 2024. Current date Apr 2026 = ~22 months post-trough. Classic timeliness window has passed.
Mitigant: DC growth re-acceleration (Q4 +62%) could represent a 'second turn' — demand-driven, distinct from the FS recovery. If so, timeliness resets.

VF modal case: FY28 EPS SEK 3.30-3.60. SS consensus ~SEK 2.20-2.50. Management guide implies ~SEK 3.00. Hierarchy holds if DC growth sustains.

sub.text: peers (8-12x). Company-driven re-rating likely if DC sustains >30% growth.

signpost.label: Multiple more company than exogenously driven?

items.text: **IC#1 CQ11 — Multiple more company-driven than exogenous?** Partially. Current de-rating was company-specific (FS margin collapse, CEO exit). Re-rating will be driven by:

sub.text: (1) DC growth confirmation (company-specific), (2) margin recovery (company-specific via PIP), (3) sector sentiment (exogenous — cable/infra re-rating). Roughly 60/40 company vs exogenous.
Company-driven: DC growth, PIP execution, M&A track record. Exogenous: hyperscaler capex cycle, EU broadband subsidies, interest rate cycle.
Prysmian trades at 8-10x EV/EBITDA vs HTRO ~4.3x. Gap is partly HTRO-specific (margin, scale, execution risk) and partly sentiment.

items.text: **IC#1 RA9 — Sufficient returns?** Yes on modal case. 3Y TSR >20% achievable through EPS growth (~20-25% CAGR) + multiple re-rating (4.3x to 8x EV/EBITDA).

sub.text: Even without re-rating, EPS growth alone delivers >20% annualised.

items.text: **IC#1 CQ12 — More than 20% 3Y TSR?** Yes on modal case. EPS CAGR 20-25% (2025-28) + multiple re-rating from 4.3x to 7-8x EV/EBITDA = 3Y TSR of 80-120% (annualised

sub.text: 22-30%). Zero dividend, so all return is capital appreciation. Downside: if DC growth stalls, TSR compresses to ~10-15% (EPS growth only, no re-rating).
Bull case 3Y TSR: ~120% (EPS 25% CAGR + re-rate to 10x). Modal case: ~80% (EPS 20% CAGR + re-rate to 8x). Bear case: ~30% (EPS 10% CAGR, no re-rating).
Current SP SEK 31.50. Modal target: SEK 55-60 (FY28 EPS SEK 3.30-3.60 x 16-17x P/E). Bull: SEK 70+.

blocks.text: Change forces are large and confirmed. External: DC hyperscaler capex explosion + EU Gigabit Act. Internal: new CEO + PIP + M&A strategy reset. Base is decent (vertically integrated manufacturer with IP in DC/HE). Main weakness: FS margin floor uncertain.

items.text: **IC#2 Required Case INPUTS:** Strong external tailwinds (DC demand explosion, EU broadband subsidies) + meaningful internal change (CEO, PIP, strategy). Base is adequate but not pristine —

sub.text: FS margin compression and 20+ acquisition integration create noise. Overall: good input quality, execution-dependent.

items.text: **IC#2 RA1 — Sufficient change forces:** Yes, both external and internal are meaningful and confirmed. External: DC bandwidth demand +330% (2020-24), hyperscaler capex acceleration. Internal:

sub.text: new CEO with McKinsey/M&A pedigree, PIP delivering SEK 110m/yr savings, strategic pivot to three-segment model.

items.text: **IC#2 CQ1 — External change forces / tailwinds?** Large and structural. (1) Hyperscaler DC capex explosion (AI/ML infrastructure). (2) EU Gigabit Infrastructure Act (force May 2024, full effect Nov 2025).

sub.text: (3) German gov EUR 38bn FTTH commitment (3x prior). (4) Defence spending increases (HE segment). Multi-year secular tailwinds.
DC bandwidth demand +330% (2020-24). EU Digital Decade: 100% Gigabit coverage by 2030. German broadband commitment EUR 38bn vs EUR 12bn prior.
External tailwinds are the strongest element of the case. DC demand is structural (AI/ML), not cyclical. EU subsidies provide FS floor.

items.text: **IC#2 CQ2 — Internal change forces?** Meaningful. New CEO Fröberg (Mar 2025) brings McKinsey + Ansell background (industrial diversification, M&A). Initiated PIP within weeks of starting.

sub.text: Strategic pivot from single-segment (FS) to three-segment model. Deputy CEO Åberg provides continuity (10+ yrs). Culture shift: from 'revenue at all costs' to 'profitable revenue'.
CEO Fröberg: McKinsey (1999-2004), Ahlsell Country Manager (2004-2010), Ansell CPMO (2010-2025). External hire signals investor confidence in strategy reset.
Internal changes are real but early. PIP execution on track (SEK 110m/yr target). M&A velocity maintained (JOWO Q2 2026). Execution risk: 20+ prior acquisitions need integration discipline.

signpost.label: Sufficient base (to build a strong case on)

items.text: **IC#2 RA2 — Sufficient base?** Adequate but not pristine. Vertically integrated manufacturer with real IP in DC/HE. Strong organic growth in DC (62% Q4). But:

sub.text: FS margin compression is a drag, leverage still 1.9x, ROCE ~10-12% vs Prysmian 20-22%. Base is 'good enough' for GARP, not blue-chip.

items.text: **IC#2 CQ3 — Absence of external headwinds?** No — headwinds present but manageable. (1) Chinese competitor pricing in FS. (2) Interest rate cycle still elevated (financing costs).

sub.text: (3) North America regulatory uncertainty (BEAD delays). (4) FS demand weakness in Europe. These are known and partially priced.
North America = 37% of revenue. BEAD program delays create near-term demand uncertainty for FS. Chinese fibre cable pricing pressure ongoing.
Headwinds are concentrated in FS (the drag segment). DC and HE face minimal external headwinds. Net: headwinds present but contained to lowest-margin segment.

items.text: **IC#2 CQ4 — Well-invested base?** Yes, reasonably. 20+ acquisitions 2020-24 built capability in DC and HE. Capex ~2-3% of revenue (maintenance + capacity).

sub.text: Rochester Cable, Tech Optics, Endor acquisitions added technical differentiation. Risk: integration of acquired assets is ongoing; some may be under-optimised.
Capex: 2-3% of revenue. Key acquisitions: Rochester Cable (electro-optical, US), Tech Optics (UK submarine), Endor (DC services).
Asset base is well-positioned for DC/HE growth. FS assets may need further rationalisation (PIP ongoing). Overall: well-invested for growth segments.

items.text: **IC#2 CQ5 — Thesis congruency with past/present?** Good. Historical narrative: acquisition-driven growth → margin expansion → strategic diversification. Present thesis (DC/HE growth + FS stabilisation) is a logical evolution.

sub.text: Not a contradictory narrative. Risk: FS was 'the growth engine' until 2024; now it's 'the drag'. Narrative shift is real but supported by data.
Congruent: company has always been an infrastructure enabler. The shift is which infrastructure segment leads growth (DC replacing FS). Not a pivot — an evolution.

items.text: **IC#2 CQ6 — Large CfC/mispricing?** Moderate. The CfC (FS margin collapse + CEO exit) was large in 2024. Most has cleared (new CEO installed, PIP underway, FS stabilising). Residual mispricing:

sub.text: market still values HTRO as a fibre cable company (EV/EBITDA 4.3x) not a DC/HE growth company (peers 8-12x). Re-rating opportunity if mix shift continues.
Current EV/EBITDA 4.3x vs Prysmian 8-10x. Discount partly justified (smaller, lower ROCE, execution risk) but partly mispricing of DC growth quality.
Mispricing is moderate, not extreme. ~50% discount to peers is too wide given DC growth trajectory. Fair discount ~20-30%. Implies 30-50% upside from re-rating alone.

items.text: **IC#2 CQ7 — Low trading multiple?** Yes. EV/EBITDA 4.3x, EV/Sales 1.0x, P/B 1.4x. All below historical average and below cable/connectivity peers. Multiple reflects trough earnings + execution risk + limited

sub.text: analyst coverage. Upside if market recognises DC/HE quality-of-earnings improvement.
Valuation: EV/EBITDA 4.3x, EV/Sales 1.0x, P/B 1.4x. Prysmian: EV/EBITDA 8-10x, EV/Sales 1.5-2.0x. Corning: 12-14x.
Multiple is low on absolute and relative basis. Discount is partly warranted (execution risk, leverage) but magnitude is excessive for a company with 62% DC organic growth.

blocks.text: Primary setup: Demand-Driven EPSU/EPT (DC hyperscaler demand explosion). Secondary: Corporate Change (CEO + PIP). Both confirmed present. Setup quality is good; DC growth is structural (AI/ML), not cyclical. No overlap with unacceptable or false friend setups.

items.text: **IC#3 Required Case SETUPS:** Primary setup (Demand-Driven EPSU/EPT) is confirmed and strong — DC +62% Q4 organic growth, hyperscaler capex structural. Secondary (Corporate Change) adds conviction.

sub.text: No overlap with unacceptable setups. Key risk: FS drag could make this a partial setup rather than clean one.

items.text: **IC#3 RA1 — Mis-modelled, high-quality EPS upgrader:** Partially present. SS consensus is likely 15-25% below VF modal case on 2027-28 EPS, driven by under-modelling DC M&A contribution and PIP run-rate.

sub.text: Gap is structural (DC growth extrapolation) rather than one-off.

signpost.label: Is EPS consensus >10% below our estimate?

items.text: **IC#3 CQ1 — Is EPS consensus >10% below our estimate?** Yes. SS consensus FY28 EPS ~SEK 2.50. VF modal case SEK 3.30-3.60. Gap is 32-44%. Driven by:

sub.text: (1) SS not modelling full DC M&A pipeline (3-6 acquisitions/yr), (2) SS using conservative DC organic growth assumptions, (3) PIP run-rate not fully reflected.
SS FY28 EPS consensus: ~SEK 2.50. VF modal: SEK 3.30-3.60. Gap: 32-44%.
Gap is meaningful and persistent. SS analysts (1-2) likely lack DC-specific expertise. Re-rating catalyst: each quarterly beat narrows the gap.

signpost.label: What specific line items are mis-modelled?

items.text: **IC#3 CQ2 — What specific line items are mis-modelled?** (1) DC revenue: SS likely models ~25% CAGR vs VF 35%+ (actual Q4 was 62%). (2) DC M&A contribution:

sub.text: SS models ~0-1 acquisitions/yr vs management target 3-6. (3) PIP savings: SS may not fully run-rate the SEK 110m/yr. (4) FS margin floor: SS may be too pessimistic (~4% vs VF 5-6%).
Biggest mis-model is DC revenue. If DC sustains >35% organic growth and adds 2-3 acquisitions/yr, revenue contribution doubles by 2028. SS consensus hasn't priced this.
DC revenue FY25: SEK 1.4bn. Management target FY28: SEK 3.0bn (+114%). SS likely models SEK 2.0-2.2bn.

signpost.label: Is the modelling gap structural or one-off?

items.text: **IC#3 CQ3 — Is the modelling gap structural or one-off?** Structural. DC demand is driven by hyperscaler capex (AI/ML), which is a multi-year secular trend.

sub.text: M&A pipeline is ongoing (3-6/yr target). PIP savings are recurring. This is not a one-quarter beat — it's a multi-year compounding gap that widens each quarter if DC growth sustains.
Structural because: (1) DC demand is secular not cyclical, (2) M&A adds cumulatively, (3) PIP is permanent cost reduction. Each quarter of sustained DC growth widens the gap.

signpost.label: Big demand change-driven EPSU or EPT

items.text: **IC#3 RA2 — Big demand change-driven EPSU or EPT:** This is the PRIMARY setup. DC hyperscaler capex explosion is large, structural, and HTRO is positioned to capture share.

sub.text: Company is not dominant but well-positioned in a growing addressable market. Fixed cost leverage exists (manufacturing scale). Market is not an oligopoly but has moderate barriers. Overall: strong demand-driven setup.

signpost.label: Is there a market-wide demand explosion?

items.text: **IC#3 CQ4 — Is there a market-wide demand explosion?** Yes, in DC segment. Hyperscaler capex on AI/ML infrastructure is driving unprecedented demand for DC cabling and connectivity.

sub.text: Bandwidth demand +330% (2020-24). HTRO DC organic growth: +37% FY25, +62% Q4 2025. This is a genuine demand explosion, not a cycle.
DC bandwidth demand +330% (2020-24). Hyperscaler capex: Meta $60bn+, Google $50bn+, Microsoft $80bn+ (2025 plans). HTRO DC: +62% Q4 organic.
Demand explosion is confirmed and structural. AI/ML training + inference workloads drive sustained cabling demand. This is the highest-conviction element of the case.

signpost.label: Does the company hold a dominant competitive position?

items.text: **IC#3 CQ5 — Does the company hold a dominant competitive position?** No, but well-positioned niche. HTRO is not a market leader (Prysmian, Corning are larger).

sub.text: But in DC connectivity specifically, HTRO has built a service-oriented model (cabling + installation + managed services) that creates stickiness. Market share is small but growing fast.
Prysmian: EUR 16bn revenue, 20-22% ROCE target. Corning: USD 14bn. HTRO: SEK 7.5bn (~USD 750m). Small relative to leaders.

Not dominant, but dominant position is not required for the thesis. HTRO's DC growth (62% organic) shows it's capturing share in a rapidly expanding market. Service model (not just hardware) creates differentiation.

signpost.label: Is the business model one-to-many scalable?

items.text: **IC#3 CQ6 — Is the business model one-to-many scalable?** Partially. DC hardware (cables, connectors) is scalable — manufacturing capacity can be expanded.

sub.text: DC services (installation, managed services) are less scalable — require local teams and project management. Hybrid model means scaling requires both capex and hiring. Not pure platform economics.
Hardware: scalable via manufacturing expansion (low capex intensity, 2-3% of revenue). Services: less scalable, requires M&A for geographic coverage. Net: moderate scalability.

signpost.label: Is the market an oligopoly (structure AND behaviour)?

items.text: **IC#3 CQ7 — Is the market an oligopoly?** No. Fibre cable market is fragmented with Chinese entrants competing on price. DC connectivity is more concentrated but not oligopolistic.

sub.text: HE/subsea is closest to oligopoly (3-4 players globally). Overall market structure is competitive, not oligopolistic. Pricing power is segment-dependent.
FS: fragmented, Chinese price competition. DC: moderate concentration (Prysmian, Corning, HTRO, Leviton + specialists). HE: concentrated (3-4 global players).
Market structure is a weakness for FS but acceptable for DC/HE. Pricing power exists in DC (service differentiation) and HE (technical barriers) but not FS.

signpost.label: Is there fixed cost leverage for margin expansion?

items.text: **IC#3 CQ8 — Is there fixed cost leverage for margin expansion?** Yes. Manufacturing has significant fixed cost base. DC at 15.3% EBITA (Q4 2025) vs FS 5.2% shows the leverage

sub.text: effect of higher-value product mix on same manufacturing infrastructure. PIP restructuring further reduces fixed cost base. Incremental DC revenue drops through at high incremental margin.
DC EBITA margin 15.3% vs group 9.4%. Every 5pp of revenue mix shift toward DC adds ~50bp to group margin.
Fixed cost leverage is confirmed and meaningful. Manufacturing, sourcing, and digital platforms scale across segments. DC growth is the highest-leverage revenue stream.

signpost.label: Strong internal changes driven EPSU / EPT

items.text: **IC#3 RA3 — Strong internal changes driven EPSU / EPT:** Secondary setup, confirmed. New CEO + PIP + strategy reset are genuine internal changes. Execution is early but on track.

sub.text: Track record mixed: 20+ acquisitions built capability but also complexity. Net: meaningful internal change, moderate conviction on execution.

items.text: **IC#3 CQ9 — Is management credible on execution?** Early to judge. CEO Fröberg has strong background (McKinsey, Ansell) but <18 months in role. Deputy CEO Åberg provides continuity.

sub.text: PIP initiated quickly (signal of decisiveness). M&A pace maintained. Board supportive. Credibility is 'cautiously positive' — need 2-3 more quarters of execution to confirm.
CEO Fröberg: appointed Mar 2025. PIP announced Q3 2025. JOWO acquisition Q2 2026. Speed of action is a positive signal.
Credibility rating: C+ (early but promising). Upgrade trigger: 2 consecutive quarters of margin improvement + successful M&A integration.

signpost.label: What is the specific internal change programme?

items.text: **IC#3 CQ10 — What is the specific internal change programme?** Three-pronged: (1) PIP: SEK 230m restructuring cost, SEK 110m/yr EBITA benefit, FS footprint consolidation (site closures/consolidation). (2) Strategic pivot:

sub.text: three-segment model with distinct P&Ls (from Q1 2025). (3) M&A acceleration: 3-6 DC acquisitions/yr, JOWO (HE, Q2 2026).
PIP: SEK 230m cost over ~12M, SEK 110m/yr recurring benefit. Target completion: end Q1 2026. Manufacturing site consolidation in Europe.
Programme is specific, measurable, and time-bound. Good internal governance signal. Risk: PIP savings may be partially offset by acquisition integration costs.

signpost.label: What is the track record of delivery so far?

items.text: **IC#3 CQ11 — What is the track record of delivery so far?** Mixed. Positive: 20+ acquisitions completed, DC built from near-zero to 20% of revenue, HE capability established. Negative:

sub.text: FS margin compression (from 15% to 5%), leverage elevated (1.9x), ROCE 10-12% vs peer 20%. New CEO's personal track record is untested at HTRO. PIP is early-stage.
Acquisition track record: 20+ deals 2020-24. DC revenue from ~0 to SEK 1.4bn. But integration remains ongoing; some assets under-optimised.
Track record is 'adequate but not excellent'. Company has demonstrated M&A capability but not yet margin discipline. PIP is the first real test of the new management's execution.

items.text: **IC#3 RA4 — Oversold/rebounding quality compounder:** Partially applicable. HTRO was oversold in 2024 (SP down >50% from highs). Rebounding now (+42% YTD). But 'quality compounder' label is a stretch —

sub.text: ROCE 10-12%, margin volatile, business quality uneven across segments. Better described as 'oversold cyclical with a growth kicker'.

signpost.label: Is the negative sentiment idiosyncratic or sector-wide?

items.text: **IC#3 CQ12 — Is the negative sentiment idiosyncratic or sector-wide?** Both. Sector-wide: European fibre cable companies faced demand weakness in 2023-24. Idiosyncratic:

sub.text: HTRO had CEO exit, FS margin collapse, leverage concerns, and limited analyst coverage. The idiosyncratic factors amplified the sector-wide weakness.
Negative sentiment was ~60% idiosyncratic (CEO exit, margin, leverage) and ~40% sector-wide (fibre demand cycle). Idiosyncratic elements are partially clearing.

signpost.label: Is the underlying business quality intact despite sentiment?

items.text: **IC#3 CQ13 — Is the underlying business quality intact?** In DC and HE: yes, quality is strong and improving (+62% DC organic, +11% HE). In FS:

sub.text: quality is impaired (margin compression, competitive pressure, commodity-like). Overall business quality is bifurcated — improving in growth segments, deteriorating in legacy.

Business quality is a tale of two companies. DC/HE = high quality (15%+ margins, structural growth, IP). FS = low quality (5% margins, competitive, cyclical). Net quality is average but trajectory is positive.

signpost.label: What specific catalyst clears the overhang?

items.text: **IC#3 CQ14 — What specific catalyst clears the overhang?** Multiple catalysts: (1) Q1-Q2 2026 earnings confirming DC growth sustained >30%. (2) PIP run-rate savings visible in reported margins.

sub.text: (3) BEAD programme disbursement (US FS demand). (4) Successful JOWO integration. (5) Any additional DC M&A at attractive terms.
Most likely near-term catalyst: Q1 2026 earnings showing DC growth sustained + margin improvement from PIP run-rate. This is expected within 1-2 quarters.

signpost.label: Huge CfC (Cause for concern) clearing event in solid quality

items.text: **IC#3 RA5 — Huge CfC clearing event in solid quality:** Partially applicable. CfC (CEO exit + FS margin collapse) is clearing but not a 'huge clearing event'.

sub.text: It's a gradual improvement, not a binary catalyst. Business quality is adequate in DC/HE but not 'solid' across the board.

signpost.label: Is there a specific clearing event identified?

items.text: **IC#3 CQ15 — Is there a specific clearing event identified?** No single clearing event. The CfC is clearing gradually: new CEO installed (Mar 2025), PIP underway, FS stabilising.

sub.text: There is no binary 'clearing event' — it's a progressive normalisation. BEAD disbursement could be the closest thing to a single catalyst for FS.
No binary clearing event. Gradual normalisation of FS + sustained DC growth = progressive CfC clearing over 3-4 quarters. Not a 'huge CfC clearing' setup.

signpost.label: Is business quality sufficient to justify rebound post-clearing?

items.text: **IC#3 CQ16 — Is business quality sufficient to justify rebound post-clearing?** Yes, in aggregate. DC business quality is high (15%+ margins, structural demand, service differentiation).

sub.text: HE is solid (11%+ margins, defence tailwinds). FS is low quality but manageable drag. Overall quality justifies a rebound to 8-10x EV/EBITDA from current 4.3x.
Quality assessment: DC = B+ (high margin, structural growth). HE = B (niche, defensible). FS = C- (commodity, low margin). Blended: C+ to B-.

items.text: **IC#3 RA6 — Mean-reverting quality business post-temporary headwind:** Partially applicable. FS headwind may not be fully temporary — structural competitive pressure from Chinese manufacturers.

sub.text: But DC/HE growth should more than offset FS drag. Not a pure mean-reversion story — it's a portfolio transformation.

items.text: **IC#3 CQ17 — Is the headwind genuinely temporary?** Mixed. DC demand weakness in 2023-24 was temporary (now recovering). FS price competition from Chinese manufacturers is structural, not temporary.

sub.text: Interest rate pressure is cyclical (easing). Net: ~60% temporary, ~40% structural. FS margin may never return to 2022-23 levels.
FS headwind is partially structural (Chinese competition, commodity pricing). DC headwind was temporary and has reversed. Net headwind assessment: improving but not fully clearing.

signpost.label: What is the normalised earnings level to revert to?

items.text: **IC#3 CQ18 — What is the normalised earnings level to revert to?** Normalised EBITA margin 10-12% (vs current 9.4% reported, 13.4% adjusted). Normalised EPS ~SEK 2.50-3.00 (FY27-28).

sub.text: This is ABOVE prior peak (SEK 3.36 in 2023) if DC/HE mix shift delivers. But FS normalised margin is 5-6%, not 15% — the 'normal' has shifted down for that segment.
FY23 peak EPS: SEK 3.36 (on 15.1% EBITA margin, heavy FS contribution). FY25: SEK 1.31 (trough). Normalised (FY27-28): SEK 2.50-3.00 on 11-12% EBITA margin.
Normalised level is below FY23 peak on margin but above on EPS due to revenue growth. Mix shift changes the composition of 'normal'.

signpost.label: Is the quality of the franchise intact through the headwind?

items.text: **IC#3 CQ19 — Is the quality of the franchise intact through the headwind?** Yes, in the segments that matter. DC franchise is strengthening (62% organic growth, service model deepening).

sub.text: HE franchise is solid (technical differentiation, defence tailwinds). FS franchise is weakened but stabilising. Overall franchise quality is intact and improving at the portfolio level.
Franchise quality is bifurcated but net positive. DC franchise is the crown jewel — growing, differentiated, high-margin. FS is the weak link but being managed.

signpost.label: Trough-on-trough turn in solid quality cyclical

items.text: **IC#3 RA7 — Trough-on-trough turn in solid quality cyclical:** Partially applicable. FS segment is cyclical and was near trough (organic -1% in 2025). DC is structural growth, not cyclical.

sub.text: HTRO is not a pure cyclical — it's a mixed portfolio. Trough-turn applies to FS specifically, not the whole company.

items.text: **IC#3 CQ20 — Are earnings genuinely at trough?** Likely yes for the overall company. FY25 EBITA margin 9.4% (reported) appears near-trough. PIP savings + DC growth should drive recovery from

sub.text: here. FS specifically may have further downside (if demand weakens further) but group earnings should have troughed.
FY25 EBITA margin 9.4% (reported) vs 15.1% peak (FY23). Q4 2025 was 7.2% (inc. restructuring). Adjusted: 13.4% suggests underlying has troughed.
Trough assessment: 80% probability that FY25 was the trough. Risk: another FS demand leg down could push FY26H1 lower. Mitigant: DC growth + PIP provide floor.

signpost.label: Is business quality sufficient to hold through the full cycle?

items.text: **IC#3 CQ21 — Is business quality sufficient to hold through the full cycle?** Yes, with caveats. Balance sheet adequate (leverage 1.9x, improving). DC and HE provide structural growth through cycles.

sub.text: FS is the cyclical risk factor. Zero dividend means no shareholder return during trough. Company can 'bridge' through FS weakness via DC/HE growth.
Holding quality: adequate. Leverage manageable (<2x), no covenant breach risk visible. DC/HE growth provides offset. Key risk: if DC demand also cycles down (unlikely near-term given AI capex).

signpost.label: What leading indicators confirm the cyclical turn?

items.text: **IC#3 CQ22 — What leading indicators confirm the cyclical turn?** (1) FS order intake stabilisation (flat to positive in Q4 2025). (2) EU broadband subsidy disbursement (German EUR 38bn commitment).

sub.text: (3) BEAD programme timeline (US). (4) Interest rate cuts easing financing costs for telco customers. (5) Inventory destocking completion in FS distribution channel.
EU Gigabit Act: full effect Nov 2025. German broadband: EUR 38bn committed. BEAD: delays but disbursement expected 2026-27. Interest rates: ECB cutting cycle ongoing.
Leading indicators are turning positive for FS. DC leading indicators (hyperscaler capex) are strongly positive. Cyclical turn for FS should confirm over next 2-3 quarters.

signpost.label: Does not overlap with unacceptable setup

items.text: **IC#3 RA8 — Does not overlap with unacceptable setup:** Confirmed. No structural decline, no broken business model, no governance red flags, no regulatory destruction.

sub.text: FS is a low-margin segment, not a broken one.

signpost.label: Does the case avoid any of the unacceptable setup patterns?

items.text: **IC#3 CQ23 — Does the case avoid unacceptable setup patterns?** Yes. No structural decline (DC/HE growing strongly). No broken business model (FS is low-margin but viable).

sub.text: No governance concerns (independent board, transparent reporting). No regulatory destruction. No value trap signals (earnings trajectory improving, not deteriorating). Clean on all unacceptable setup screens.
Screened against all unacceptable patterns: structural decline, broken model, governance, regulatory, value trap. Clear on all.

signpost.label: Does not overlap with false friend setup

items.text: **IC#3 RA9 — Does not overlap with false friend setup:** Mostly clear. Minor risk: FS segment could be a 'false friend' if margin recovery stalls and management attention shifts from

sub.text: DC to FS firefighting. But DC growth trajectory is strong enough to dominate the portfolio outcome.

signpost.label: Does the case avoid any of the false friend setup patterns?

items.text: **IC#3 CQ24 — Does the case avoid false friend patterns?** Mostly yes. Not a 'cheap for a reason' — valuation discount is excessive for DC growth quality.

sub.text: Not a 'cyclical masquerading as structural' — DC demand is genuinely structural (AI/ML). Minor false friend risk: FS could absorb management bandwidth, diluting DC execution. Acceptable risk at portfolio level.
False friend screen: mostly clear. Only FS-related drag risk is a minor concern. DC thesis is genuinely structural, not a false friend cyclical.

blocks.text: Building blocks (P4) at ESA — Quality of business, moat, and operator/allocator assessment. HTRO has a bifurcated quality profile: DC/HE are high-quality growth segments, FS is a low-quality legacy. Overall building block quality: C+.

items.text: **P4 CQ1 — Business quality assessment:** Bifurcated. DC segment (20% of revenue, 37% of adjusted EBITA) is high quality: 15%+ margins, structural demand, service differentiation, recurring customer relationships.

sub.text: HE is solid: technical barriers, defence tailwinds, 11%+ margins. FS is low quality: 5% margins, commodity pricing, Chinese competition.
Segment quality: DC = B+ (high margin, structural growth). HE = B (niche, defensible). FS = C- (commodity, low margin).
Blended quality is C+ and improving — trajectory is toward DC/HE dominance, which lifts portfolio quality over 2-3 years.

items.text: **P4 CQ2 — Competitive moat / BtE:** Moderate. DC: service-oriented model (cabling + installation + managed services) creates switching costs. HE: technical certifications and specialised manufacturing create barriers. FS:

sub.text: low moat, commodity market, limited differentiation. Overall moat is narrow but deepening in growth segments.
Moat assessment: DC moat widening (service stickiness). HE moat stable (technical barriers). FS moat narrow and possibly eroding.

items.text: **P4 CQ3 — Operator / allocator quality:** Early to judge under new CEO. Capital allocation improving: PIP shows cost discipline, M&A shifting to higher-ROCE segments (DC, HE).

sub.text: Zero dividend is appropriate given leverage. 20+ acquisitions show execution capability but also complexity risk. Fröberg's McKinsey/Ansell background suggests structured approach.
Capital allocation: zero dividend (appropriate), 3-6 acquisitions/yr target (DC focused), PIP (cost discipline). ROCE ~10-12% (below peers).
Operator quality: C+ (early but improving). Key test: JOWO integration + next 2-3 DC acquisitions. Need to see ROCE improvement toward 15%+ by FY27-28.

blocks.text: _Fundamental case rates C+ overall. DC growth is real, change forces confirmed, and valuation attractive. The binding constraints are DC moat durability and M&A execution quality — both require ESA/DD depth to resolve._
_AI/DC infrastructure is fully paradigm-aligned. Defence/HE fits risk-off positioning. FTTH legacy is paradigm-negative but being diluted by mix shift._

items.text: **Paradigm 1 (Late bull/early bear): C+.** Mixed — DC/HE are defensive growth; FTTH cyclically exposed.
**Paradigm 2 (AI demand): B+.** Directly paradigm-aligned — DC fibre is the physical layer of AI infrastructure buildout.
**Paradigm 3 (Oil/stagflation): B-.** No Iran exposure. HE/defence benefits from geopolitical tensions. Tariff risk limited.

blocks.text: _Net paradigm fit is positive. The AI/DC infrastructure alignment is the strongest paradigm attribute. FTTH legacy is the drag._
_Limited coverage (4 analysts) but revisions turning positive post-Q4 beat. Kepler Hold and Danske Sell are potential converts._

blocks.title: P5 SS Earnings Momentum — 5 dimensions x 3 timeframes (HTRO consensus snapshot)

D.title: COMPONENTS
D.inline_verdict: 10 INVALIDATION ACHs armed with HTRO-specific triggers; 3 KOQs for ESA

subsections.title: KDICs — Key Drivers of Investment Case

items.text: **FULCRUM #1: DC segment competitive moat durability** — Can HTRO's mid-market DC services niche sustain 20%+ organic growth and 15%+ margins against Corning/Prysmian pivot? KD Rating: YELLOW.

**FULCRUM #2: M&A execution quality** — Requires 3-6 acquisitions/year at 5-7x EBITDA with successful integration. Weterings closure (acquired 2021 for EUR 5.6m, closed 2025) is a capital destruction flag.

sub.text: KD Rating: ORANGE.

items.text: **KEY DRIVER #3: FTTH recovery trajectory** — BEAD impact timing (H2-26 per mgmt, 2027 per SS). Fibre Solutions margin rebound from 6.4% to 10% target. KD Rating: YELLOW.
**KEY DRIVER #4: CEO quality (Fröberg)** — 12 months in; conservative guidance reset + Q3-Q4 beats. M&A background (Ahlsell) is relevant. Untested in fibre industry downturn. KD Rating: YELLOW.
**KEY DRIVER #5: EPS bridge to 2028** — SEK 515m EBITA (7.2% margin, 2025) → SEK 1.32bn EBITA (12%, 2028). 2.6x growth required.

sub.text: Achievable if DC sustains + M&A executes + costs land. KD Rating: YELLOW.

items.text: **KHF-1: Balance sheet health.** Net debt SEK 1,582m, leverage 1.9x EBITDA. Covenant headroom adequate. No immediate refinancing risk.
**KHF-2: Cash conversion.** Q4-25 operating cash flow conversion 235%. Strong WC management (inventory -SEK 144m, AR -SEK 94m). Payback on restructuring ~1.1 years.
**KHF-3: Governance.** No controlling shareholder (largest: Handelsbanken Fonder 9.3%). Board + mgmt own ~13%. Erik Selin related-party lease (SEK 7m/year) is minor. No red flags.
**KA-1: Assumption — hyperscaler capex sustains.** Modal case assumes 15% CAGR in cloud DC market over N5Y. If AI monetisation disappoints, capex could pause (bear case trigger).
**KA-2: Assumption — HTRO can acquire at 5-7x EBITDA.** If PE buyers inflate DC service company multiples, M&A engine stalls.
**KA-3: Assumption — Fibre Solutions stabilises at 10% EBITA.** Requires cost programme delivery + BEAD-driven volume recovery. If overcapacity persists, margin stays at 6-7%.
**KC-1: DC switching costs.** No disclosed customer lock-in periods. Microduct is not proprietary — CommMesh, Corning, others offer similar systems. 'Flexibility' helps the customer, not HTRO.

sub.text: Is the moat real or perceived?

items.text: **KC-2: M&A integration model.** Decentralised integration (preserving entrepreneurial culture) works at small scale. Does it scale to 12+ acquisitions over 3 years without creating coordination failure?
**KC-3: FTTH structural floor.** Is the FTTH business permanently impaired (negative terminal growth per Redeye bear case) or cyclically depressed with secular catch-up in US/UK/Germany?

**KR-1: DC boom-bust risk.** Redeye explicitly warns DC hyperscaler demand shares FTTH boom-bust characteristics. If capex pauses, HTRO's DC segment could decelerate from +37% to <10% within 2-3 quarters. Probability:

sub.text: 20-30% over N2Y.

items.text: **KR-2: Customer concentration.** Top 10 = ~50% of sales. Loss or procurement shift by 1-2 major hyperscalers could cause a revenue miss of 5-10%.
**KR-3: Corning/Prysmian competitive pivot.** If Tier-1 carrier demand stalls, these giants redirect excess capacity and salesforce to Tier-2/3 alt-nets — HTRO's core customer base.

sub.text: Scale advantage is overwhelming in a price war.

items.text: **KR-4: CEO execution risk.** Fröberg lacks fibre industry experience. 12 months is insufficient to judge turnaround capability in a structurally challenged segment.
**KR-5: FX headwinds.** Strong SEK vs USD/EUR erodes translated revenue. 9% negative FX impact on sales in late 2025.
**KR-6: Glass preform supply vulnerability.** Not vertically integrated. Relies on third-party glass producers. If synchronised demand boom hits, supply shortages could cap revenue growth.

**KR-7: LEO satellite substitution.** Starlink/Amazon Kuiper receiving BEAD funding eligibility. Gen-3 satellites at 1 Tbit/s could narrow fibre performance gap in rural/remote markets, capping FTTH TAM.
**KPO-1: BEAD acceleration.** If US fibre deployment ramps faster than expected, Fibre Solutions could recover to mid-teens margins, adding SEK 200-300m incremental EBITA.
**KPO-2: Edge DC proliferation.** Next wave of infrastructure (distributed edge DCs closer to end users) could extend DC growth runway beyond 2028.

sub.text: HTRO's local service model is well-positioned for fragmented edge deployment.

items.text: **KPO-3: Multiple re-rating on mix shift.** As DC/HE reaches 50% of revenue, the blended multiple should re-rate from FTTH-legacy 10-12x toward services 15-18x.

sub.text: This is the biggest source of TSR asymmetry.

items.text: **KPO-4: Defence budget escalation.** Geopolitical tensions (Iran, Russia) could accelerate NATO defence spending beyond 2% commitments, providing additional HE segment tailwind.

blocks.text: _{10_INVALIDATION_ACHS} **Live-case invalidation discipline.** These ten one-strike rules apply __while we own this case__ and are explicitly different from the screening thresholds used for new investments. Any single trip → __APM escalates to Richard__ with a sell/trim recommendation; no waiting for a second confirmation. State of each threshold is tracked below._
**Single-event invalidations (rules 1–5)** — fundamental triggers that fire on a single condition.

items.text: **1. Top-line invalidation** — One __probable or actual near-term revenue cut__ to SS/G caused by an exogenous problem. HTRO-specific: DC segment organic growth drops below +10% for two consecutive quarters,

sub.text: OR Fibre Solutions revenue declines >15% YoY without BEAD offset.

items.text: **2. Cockroach invalidation** — __One actual AND one probable__, OR __three probable/likely__ current/near-term problems. HTRO-specific: (i) M&A write-down (second Weterings-type capital destruction), (ii) DC customer loss (top-3 hyperscaler terminates or

sub.text: significantly reduces procurement), (iii) FTTH overcapacity worsens despite cost programme.

items.text: **3. Ditherer invalidation** — __Deterioration in operator assessment__ AND one or more problems. HTRO-specific: CEO Fröberg loses credibility (guidance miss >20%, strategic reversal on DC pivot, unexplained departure of key

sub.text: DC segment leaders) AND concurrent FTTH deterioration or DC deceleration.

items.text: **4. Cyclical invalidation** — __SP underperformance of 15% or 3M__ FOLLOWED BY a plausible+ near-term revenue cut caused by threshold+ exogenous problem. HTRO-specific:

sub.text: SP drops below SEK 24 (15% from ~SEK 28) FOLLOWED BY hyperscaler capex freeze or BEAD programme cancellation/major reallocation to satellite.

items.text: **5. NT/MT one-two invalidation** — __SP underperformance of 15% or 3M__ FOLLOWED BY any probable/actual near-term profit cut. HTRO-specific: SP drops below SEK 24 FOLLOWED BY Fibre Solutions margin falling

sub.text: below 5% or DC margin contracting below 12% (profitability squeeze from either segment).

blocks.text: **SP-conditional & composite invalidations (rules 6–10)** — sequence-dependent triggers (price action × fundamentals).

items.text: **6. Wisdom of crowd invalidation** — __SP underperformance of 15% or 3M__ FOLLOWED BY any plausible+ VF or actual SM threshold+ concerns re. __mid/long-term growth, margins, SRCAs__ or predictability thereof.

sub.text: HTRO-specific: SP <SEK 24 FOLLOWED BY evidence that DC services lack sustainable competitive advantage (e.g., Corning/Prysmian successfully enter mid-market DC services at scale, margin compression across DC peer group).

items.text: **7. Market catch-up with our existential concerns invalidation** — __VF plausible+ threshold+ concerns__ re. mid/long-term growth/margins/SRCAs FOLLOWED BY __SP underperformance of 15% or 3M__. HTRO-specific:

sub.text: Watson identifies DC boom-bust pattern confirmation (e.g., hyperscaler order cancellations, DC services pricing deflation >10%) FOLLOWED BY SP <SEK 24.

items.text: **8. Narrow frame invalidation** — __Peerset underperformance of 15% or 3M__ FOLLOWED BY any __actual cut/problem__. HTRO-specific: HTRO underperforms Nordic mid-cap industrials peer group by >15% FOLLOWED BY actual earnings

sub.text: cut, M&A write-down, or customer loss.

items.text: **9. SS EEG invalidation** — __[2]% or greater SS EPS cuts__ AND __SP underperformance of 15% or 3M__ (either order). HTRO-specific: consensus EPS cut of ≥2% (i.e., from SEK 2.25

sub.text: to SEK 2.20 or below) combined with SP <SEK 24.

items.text: **10. Case outputs/attributes invalidation** — __[8] or more case-output thresholds at D or F__. HTRO-specific: if 8+ of the IC#1 output CQs/TCs degrade to D or F ratings (e.g., EPS

sub.text: growth <5%, no upgrade path, multiple de-rates to <10x, case navigability collapses), the thesis is fundamentally broken regardless of SP.

blocks.text: _Escalation protocol: when a threshold trips, APM (i) records the trip in the case file with date and evidence, (ii) re-rates the affected pillar(s), (iii) escalates with __trigger evidence, thesis impact, recommended action__ (sell / trim X% / hold-with-tighter-stop) and reasoning chain, and (iv) flips the state in this section to TRIPPED. This sits __upstream of__ the 30-Day Shot Clock — the shot clock applies for deterioration without a tripped threshold; once a threshold trips, APM acts._

**Plausible invalidation scenarios (not yet ACH-level, but watchlist):**

(i) AI monetisation disappointment — if hyperscaler revenue growth from AI products disappoints (e.g., Meta/Google AI ad revenue plateaus), capex could be cut 20-30%, directly impacting DC fibre demand. Plausibility: 15-20% over N2Y.

(ii) FTTH permanent impairment — if North American FTTH demand never recovers to 2022 levels and BEAD is substantially reallocated to satellite/FWA, Fibre Solutions becomes a structurally declining business. Bear case terminal margin: 4-5% EBIT. Plausibility: 25-30% over N3Y.

(iii) M&A multiple inflation — PE competition for DC service companies inflates acquisition multiples to 10-12x EBITDA, making HTRO's 5-7x target unachievable and stalling the inorganic growth engine. Plausibility: 20% over N2Y.

**Short seller coverage:** No documented short-seller campaigns from major activist firms. No specific short interest data available. Shareholder base: institutional long-only dominated (Handelsbanken Fonder 9.3%, Accendo Capital 5.9%, pension funds).

**ESA-depth bear case assessment: ORANGE credibility.** The bear case is fundamentally operational, not accounting-driven: (i) FTTH boom-bust is confirmed fact — profit warning July 2025, SEK 299m restructuring charges, Fibre Solutions EBITA collapsed to 5.2% in Q4-25. (ii) DC boom-bust is speculative but credible — Redeye explicitly warns; hyperscaler capex patterns are historically volatile. (iii) Redeye bear case models SEK 12-15/share (40-50% downside), terminal growth -2% to -3%, EBIT margin 4-5%. (iv) Quality of earnings concern: non-GAAP adjustments increasing — SEK 202m in Q3-25, SEK 97m in Q4-25. Adjusted vs reported EBITA gap widening.

**ESA counter-assessment:** (i) DC segment is accelerating, not contracting — directly contradicts bear thesis at current juncture. (ii) Cash conversion is exceptional (235% Q4-25) — suggests earnings quality is understated, not overstated. (iii) Non-GAAP adjustments are explicable (one-time restructuring of known operations) — not suspicious. (iv) No governance red flags (related-party lease is SEK 7m/year, immaterial). (v) Historical ROIC volatility is a legitimate structural concern but reflects FTTH cyclicality, not management fraud. **Net assessment: bears have a plausible downside scenario but no smoking gun. The bet against HTRO is a cyclicality bet, not an integrity bet.**

items.text: **KOQ-1: DC switching costs — real or perceived?** Need ESA-depth evidence of customer lock-in: contract lengths, proprietary integration, service dependency, reference customer interviews. This is the moat question.
**KOQ-2: M&A pipeline quality.** What is the actual pipeline? How many targets identified? At what multiples? What is the integration failure rate historically? Weterings is a flag —

sub.text: is it an outlier or a pattern?

items.text: **KOQ-3: Fröberg's DC expertise.** Does the CEO understand the DC services market specifically, or is he applying an industrial wholesale playbook (Ahlsell) to a technology services market? What is his

sub.text: view on the boom-bust risk?

items.text: **KA-1: Monitor Q1-26 DC organic print (April 2026).** >+30% = conviction-building → consider upsize to 2.0%. <+15% = KC-1 moat concern escalates → hold size, re-assess at Q2.
**KA-2: Track first post-Fröberg M&A deal.** When announced: check (i) target EV/EBITDA multiple, (ii) strategic fit (DC services? geography?), (iii) funding (cash flow funded or debt-funded?).

sub.text: Compare to Weterings as calibration.

items.text: **KA-3: Run Minervini 8-point technical.** Confirm Stage 1→2 transition. Set alerts at SEK 24 (ACH zone) and SEK 32 (breakout).
**KA-4: Check Corning/Prysmian Q1-26 results for mid-market commentary.** If either signals pivot to Tier-2/3 DC services, KC-1 moat risk escalates.
**KA-5: Check BEAD state allocation data.** Track % allocated to fibre vs satellite/FWA. If fibre share drops below 60%, KR-7 LEO substitution risk escalates.

**KA-6: Request Richard track-record check.** Any parallels with Fasadgruppen/XVIVO on Nordic mid-cap illiquidity? Market cap SEK 5.8bn is above XVIVO but liquidity gate should be explicitly assessed.

subsections.title: Pros/cons of prioritising vs other opportunities

blocks.text:
**Pros of prioritising HTRO:** (i) Genuine asymmetry — 60-100% upside on modal case vs 40-50% downside on bear case at starter size. (ii) AI/DC paradigm alignment — rare in European mid-cap space. (iii) Fulcrum event inside 12m (Q1-26 DC print). (iv) Counter-cyclical to portfolio if positioned in risk-off sectors.

**Cons:** (i) Below $1.5bn market cap — liquidity constraint for larger sizing. (ii) M&A execution risk is hard to assess remotely. (iii) FTTH legacy creates headline risk even if DC thesis is correct. (iv) Competes for capital with PRY/NKT cables theme which has better scale/vertical integration.

**Net assessment:** Justified at starter position (1.0-1.5%). Do not prioritise over higher-conviction pipeline names (DCC, CARLB) for capital allocation.

**Trackable Indicators (TIs):** (i) DC segment organic growth (quarterly — must sustain >25% to confirm thesis). (ii) DC EBITA margin (quarterly — must stay >12% to confirm unit economics). (iii) Group M&A activity (event-driven — check multiples, strategic fit, integration). (iv) Fibre Solutions margin trajectory (quarterly — cost programme delivery check).

**Investment Case Drivers (ICDs):** (i) Hyperscaler capex announcements (Meta, Microsoft, Google quarterly results). (ii) BEAD state allocation data (quarterly NTIA updates). (iii) Corning/Prysmian quarterly commentary on DC services and mid-market pivot.

**Peer monitoring:** Corning (GLW), Prysmian (PRY), CommScope (COMM), NKT. Track for competitive dynamics, pricing, capacity.

subsections.title: KQs/KAs if/when progressed to next stage

blocks.text:
**Key Questions for DD P1:** (i) DC switching costs — contract lengths, customer lock-in mechanisms, reference interviews. (ii) M&A pipeline — detailed target list, multiples, integration failure rate. (iii) Financial model — build full 3-statement model with segment-level detail. (iv) Fröberg deep-dive — management interviews, board assessment.

**Key Actions for DD P1:** (i) Build proprietary financial model (segment P&L, consolidated FCF, balance sheet). (ii) Map DC services competitive landscape at ESA+ depth. (iii) Run Corning/Prysmian competitive analysis — are they pivoting to mid-market? (iv) Assess BEAD allocation data for fibre vs satellite/FWA trends.

**Would park if:** (i) DC organic growth drops below +15% for one quarter — suggests momentum fading. (ii) Corning/Prysmian successfully enter mid-market DC services at scale — moat collapses. (iii) Second M&A write-down (Weterings pattern repeats) — execution credibility destroyed. (iv) FTTH overcapacity worsens despite cost programme — structural impairment confirmed.

**Gaps remaining after ESA:** DC switching costs not evidenced at customer level. M&A integration model untested at scale (12+ deals over 3 years). Financial model not built.

**Re-activate if:** (i) DC organic sustains >30% for 4 consecutive quarters — confirms structural demand, not one-off. (ii) M&A track record improves — 3+ post-Fröberg deals with disclosed RONIC above 15%. (iii) FTTH recovery materialises — BEAD volumes visible in Fibre Solutions revenue. (iv) Stage 2 technical breakout confirmed — MAs align, RS Rank >70.

**Monitoring cadence if parked:** (i) Quarterly: HTRO earnings for DC organic growth + segment margins. (ii) Quarterly: Corning/Prysmian results for DC services competitive dynamics. (iii) Monthly: BEAD state allocation data from NTIA. (iv) Event-driven: M&A announcements for deal quality assessment. (v) Weekly: FactSet price alerts at SEK 24 (ACH zone) and SEK 32 (breakout).
